observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review and analyze the journal entries made in the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts under the General Fund, Special Education Fund and Trust Fund in order to identify the appropriate fund to which those amounts are payable or receivable and effect promptly the necessary correcting entries in the books of accounts. We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary journal entry to reclassify the funds received for calamity assistance totalling P10,250,000.00 from the Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs accounts to Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account and record future financial assistance received for disaster related purposes in the Trust Liabilities-DRRMF account pursuant to COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
Draw necessary adjusting entries to return the unexpended Capital Outlay portion of the LDRRMF from the Special Trust Fund to the General Fund, amounting to P2,884.08;
Transfer only the portion of unexpended LDRRMF classified as MOOE in the Special Trust Fund in accordance with Items 5.1.10 and 5.1.11 of COA Circular No. 2012 	6002; and
Review the discrepancy of P47,688.00 and draw adjusting entries, if necessary, to reconcile the balance presented in the Statement of Financial Position against the Report of Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF.
We also recommended that the Head of the MDRRMO, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer, appropriate the savings from excess balances of completed programs/projects from Capital Outlay for other DRRM-related activities of the LGU.

We recommended that the Management strictly follow the computation of employer share as provided under Rule VI, Section I of the IRR of R.A. 9679 or the Home Development Mutual Fund Law of 2009, and stop the payment of excessive contributions.

We recommended that the Management:

Coordinate with the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer to carefully plan and schedule each project’s implementation and to ensure timely implementation and completion of development projects; and
Revert all savings from completed projects to the unappropriated surplus, which shall be available for use for other expenditures or priority development projects of the LGU.

We recommended that the Management, henceforth, comply with Section 62.1 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 (Section 90.1 of the IRR of RA No. 12009) by either: a) deducting a retention money in an amount equivalent to at least one percent but not to exceed five percent from every progress payment to the supplier; or b) requiring the submission of a special bank guarantee equivalent to at least one percent but not to exceed five percent of the total contract price, to ensure that the LGU is afforded the appropriate protection in the event of manufacturing defects in goods delivered under procurement contracts of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant reconcile the cashbook balance with the accounting records at least quarterly in accordance with the aforementioned regulations and immediately record the necessary correcting entries to ensure that the balances therein of the Cash in Local Treasury are reconciled.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer conduct a thorough review of the internal control procedures on the collections and remittances in order to identify weaknesses therein and take appropriate action to strengthen them. Moreover, we recommended that the Liquidating Officer implement stricter measures in the remittances of collections to ensure that the amount remitted to her is equal to the amount of the collecting officer’s collections.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to undertake the following:
A. Require the concerned barangays to submit immediately the liquidation reports for the previous years’ and current year’s fund transfers amounting to P2,359,873.08 and P1,154,500.00, respectively;
B. Continue the strict monitoring of the submission of liquidation reports and the issuance of demand letters to concerned barangays who failed to comply therewith. This will ensure immediate submission of liquidation reports after the purpose/s for which it was granted has been served or completed; and
C. Prohibit the payment of fund transfer unless the required certification that previous fund transfers have already been liquidated or accounted for, thus avoiding accumulation of past due accounts pursuant to Item 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review and analyze the journal entries made in the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts under the General Fund, Special Education Fund and Trust Fund in order to identify the appropriate fund to which those amounts are payable or receivable and effect promptly the necessary correcting entries in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Management:
Continue the implementation of the projects funded under the 20% Development Fund and show their commitment in serving the constituents of the Municipality;
Inquire from the DENR the status of the Environmental Compliance Certificate for its Sanitary Landfill project to facilitate its immediate approval; and
Revert all savings from completed projects to the unappropriated surplus, which shall be available for use for other expenditures or priority development projects of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer, in coordination with the Head of the MDRRMO, conduct a thorough review and evaluation of the programs, projects, and activities contained in the LDRRMFIP to ensure that appropriations thereto are appropriately classified as Capital Outlay or MOOE.  The unexpended balances of the current year LDRRMF classified as MOOE shall be transferred in the Special Trust Fund while the unexpended Capital Outlay shall remain in the General Fund.

Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw the appropriate adjusting entry to return the balance of P98,920.00 from the Trust Fund to the General Fund books.

We recommended that the Management implement corrective measures and/or conduct repairs on the noted scaling of road pavement in the Concreting of Access Road at Brgy. New Orlins, Lasam and Concreting of Barangay Road at Brgy. Nabannagan, Lasam projects.

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Engineer submit a Statement of Work Accomplished for the Improvement of Lasam Municipal Hall Phase II project as of June 21, 2022 in order to facilitate the computation of liquidated damages for the 107 days delay in its completion.

As for the Desilting of Tucalan Creek at Brgy. San Pedro, Lasam project, we recommended that the Management perform desilting on some portions of the creek that were already silted.

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Municipal Engineering Office to supervise the proper implementation of the infrastructure projects to avoid any deficiency in the infrastructure projects of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Management require Powertrac Incorporated to submit a special bank guarantee equivalent to at least one percent (1%) of the total contract price, which will cover the warranty obligation of the supplier, in accordance with Section 62.1 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations.

We recommended that the Management ensure that warranty securities posted by contractors in procurement projects of the Municipality conform to the forms and amounts prescribed under Section 62.2.3.3 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184 to ensure that it is protected against possible failures of contractors to comply with its responsibilities in the event of structural defects in infrastructure projects.

We recommended that the Management strictly follow the imposition of the ten percent (10%) retention money on progress payments to contractors in accordance with Item 6.1 of Annex E of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184.  Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that the disbursements for the release of the aforementioned retention money are supported with the documents required in Item 9.1.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the Management, henceforth, require the winning bidders for the procurement of infrastructure projects of the LGU to submit their Construction Safety and Health Program duly approved by the Department of Labor and Employment pursuant to Section 37.2.3 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and Department Order No. 13, series of 1998 of DOLE.

We recommended that the Management require the BAC, through its Secretariat, to post the Notice of Award, approved Contract Agreement and Notice to Proceed of all its procurement thru public bidding in the PhilGEPS and agency website within three (3) days and fifteen (15) days, respectively, in compliance with the Sections 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
